query: /* select#1 */ select `testdb`.`orders`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`orders`.`user_id` AS `user_id`,`testdb`.`orders`.`status` AS `status`,`testdb`.`orders`.`total` AS `total`,`testdb`.`orders`.`created_at` AS `created_at` from `testdb`.`orders` where ((`testdb`.`orders`.`status` = 'delivered') and (`testdb`.`orders`.`total` > 500.00))

inputs.operation: Index lookup on orders using idx_status (status='delivered'), with index condition: (orders.`status` = 'delivered')
